__comment: Hand-written CanMV stub signatures. These entries override or extend generated .pyi files for better VSCode/Pylance argument tips.

digest.doc: Return the binary digest and finalize this hash object.

update.doc: Update the hash object with bytes-like data.

digest.doc: Return the binary digest and finalize this hash object.

update.doc: Update the hash object with bytes-like data.

digest.doc: Return the binary digest and finalize this hash object.

update.doc: Update the hash object with bytes-like data.

get_pin_cfg.doc: Return the current FPIOA configuration object for a pin.

set_function.doc: Assign an FPIOA function to a physical pin.
set_function.signature: (self, pin: int, func: int, *, ie: int = ..., oe: int = ..., pu: int = ..., pd: int = ..., ds: int = ..., st: int = ..., sl: int = ...) -> None

Pin.init: (self, id: int, mode: int = ..., pull: int = ..., *, value: int = ..., drive: int = ..., alt: int = ...) -> None

init.doc: Configure pin mode, pull, initial output value and drive strength.
init.signature: (self, mode: int = ..., pull: int = ..., *, value: int = ..., drive: int = ..., alt: int = ...) -> None

irq.doc: Configure an interrupt callback for this pin.
irq.signature: (self, handler: Any = ..., trigger: int = ..., hard: bool = ...) -> Any

mode.doc: Read or set the pin mode.

pull.doc: Read or set the pin pull configuration.

value.doc: Read the pin value, or set it when value is provided.

UART.init: (self, id: int, baudrate: int = 115200, bits: int = 8, parity: int = ..., stop: int = ..., *, timeout: int = 10, tx: Optional[int] = None, rx: Optional[int] = None) -> None

init.doc: Configure baudrate, frame format, timeout and optional FPIOA TX/RX pins.
init.signature: (self, baudrate: int = 115200, bits: int = 8, parity: int = ..., stop: int = ..., *, timeout: int = 10, tx: Optional[int] = None, rx: Optional[int] = None) -> None

readinto.signature: (self, buf: Any, nbytes: Optional[int] = None) -> int

bootloader.doc: Reboot into bootloader mode when supported by the board.

mem_copy.doc: Copy length bytes from source address to destination address.
mem_copy.signature: (dst: int, src: int, length: int) -> None

temperature.doc: Read the chip temperature in degrees Celsius.

PWM.init: (self, channel_or_pin: int, *, freq: int = -1, duty: int = -1, duty_u16: int = -1, duty_ns: int = -1, invert: bool = False) -> None
PWM.init_doc: Create a PWM object from a PWM channel or supported pin.

init.doc: Update PWM frequency and duty settings.
init.signature: (self, *, freq: int = -1, duty: int = -1, duty_u16: int = -1, duty_ns: int = -1, invert: bool = False) -> None
(self, baudrate: int = 500000, *, polarity: int = 0, phase: int = 0, bits: int = 8, cs: int = -1, data_line: int = 1, firstbit: int = -1, sck: Any = ..., mosi: Any = ..., miso: Any = ...) -> None
(self, mode: int = ..., freq: int = -1, period: int = -1, callback: Any = None) -> None

run.doc: Run the FFT and return complex output pairs.

init.doc: Unsupported on this CanMV port; construct a new I2C object instead.

Sensor.init: (self, *, id: int = ..., type: Optional[int] = None, force: bool = False, fps: int = 60, width: int = 1920, height: int = 1080) -> None

set_framesize.doc: Set output frame size by preset, w/h, or width/height keyword arguments.
set_framesize.signature: (self, framesize: int = ..., chn: int = ..., alignment: int = ..., crop: bool = ..., *, w: int = ..., h: int = ..., width: int = ..., height: int = ...) -> None

set_pixformat.doc: Set output pixel format, for example Sensor.RGB565 or Sensor.YUV420SP.

snapshot.doc: Capture and return an image/frame from the selected channel.
snapshot.signature: (self, chn: int = ..., timeout: int = ..., dump_frame: bool = ..., auto_reboot: bool = ...) -> Any

Keyboard.init: (self, path: Optional[str] = None, timeout_ms: int = 300, auto_reconnect: bool = True) -> None

info.doc: Return device information such as kind, name, path and capability bits.

is_open.doc: Return True when the HID device is connected.

open.doc: Open the HID device, optionally overriding the input event path.

read.doc: Read one keyboard frame. The result includes events, chars, text and modifier states.

reconnect.doc: Try to reconnect after the device was unplugged.

Mouse.init: (self, path: Optional[str] = None, timeout_ms: int = 300, auto_reconnect: bool = True) -> None

info.doc: Return device information such as kind, name, path and capability bits.

is_open.doc: Return True when the HID device is connected.

open.doc: Open the HID device, optionally overriding the input event path.

read.doc: Read one mouse/touch frame. The result includes relative, absolute, wheel and button state fields.

reconnect.doc: Try to reconnect after the device was unplugged.

Serial.init: (self, path: Optional[str] = None, timeout_ms: int = 300) -> None

readinto.signature: (self, buf: Any, nbytes: Optional[int] = None) -> int

Touch.init: (self, path: Optional[str] = None, timeout_ms: int = 300, auto_reconnect: bool = True) -> None

info.doc: Return device information such as kind, name, path and capability bits.

is_open.doc: Return True when the HID device is connected.

open.doc: Open the HID device, optionally overriding the input event path.

read.doc: Read one mouse/touch frame. The result includes relative, absolute, wheel and button state fields.

reconnect.doc: Try to reconnect after the device was unplugged.

bind_layer.doc: Bind an MPP source channel to a display layer.
bind_layer.signature: (src: Any, *, rect: Any = None, pix_format: int = -1, layer: int = -1, alpha: int = 255, flag: int = 0) -> Any

writeback.doc: Read or set display writeback state.

snapshot.doc: Capture one frame from the selected UVC video mode.

video_mode.doc: Return the current UVC mode, or create a mode from width, height, fourcc and fps.
video_mode.signature: (width: int = ..., height: int = ..., fourcc: int = ..., fps: int = ...) -> Any

mask_circle.doc: Apply a circular mask to the image in place.
mask_circle.signature: (self, cx: int = ..., cy: int = ..., cr: int = ...) -> Any

mask_ellipse.doc: Apply an elliptical mask to the image in place.
mask_ellipse.signature: (self, cx: int = ..., cy: int = ..., rx: int = ..., ry: int = ..., r: int = 0) -> Any

mask_rectangle.doc: Apply a rectangular mask to the image in place.
mask_rectangle.signature: (self, rx: int = ..., ry: int = ..., rw: int = ..., rh: int = ...) -> Any

mean_pooled.doc: Return a new image reduced by mean pooling.
mean_pooled.signature: (self, x_div: int, y_div: int, *, alloc: int = ..., cache: bool = ..., data: Any = ...) -> Any

rgb_to_binary.doc: Convert RGB components using rgb to binary.
rgb_to_binary.signature: (r: int, g: int, b: int) -> Any

rgb_to_grayscale.doc: Convert RGB components using rgb to grayscale.
rgb_to_grayscale.signature: (r: int, g: int, b: int) -> Any

rgb_to_lab.doc: Convert RGB components using rgb to lab.
rgb_to_lab.signature: (r: int, g: int, b: int) -> Any

rgb_to_yuv.doc: Convert RGB components using rgb to yuv.
rgb_to_yuv.signature: (r: int, g: int, b: int) -> Any

lab_to_binary.doc: Convert LAB components using lab to binary.
lab_to_binary.signature: (l: int, a: int, b: int) -> Any

lab_to_grayscale.doc: Convert LAB components using lab to grayscale.
lab_to_grayscale.signature: (l: int, a: int, b: int) -> Any

lab_to_rgb.doc: Convert LAB components using lab to rgb.
lab_to_rgb.signature: (l: int, a: int, b: int) -> Any

lab_to_yuv.doc: Convert LAB components using lab to yuv.
lab_to_yuv.signature: (l: int, a: int, b: int) -> Any

yuv_to_binary.doc: Convert YUV components using yuv to binary.
yuv_to_binary.signature: (y: int, u: int, v: int) -> Any

yuv_to_grayscale.doc: Convert YUV components using yuv to grayscale.
yuv_to_grayscale.signature: (y: int, u: int, v: int) -> Any

yuv_to_lab.doc: Convert YUV components using yuv to lab.
yuv_to_lab.signature: (y: int, u: int, v: int) -> Any

yuv_to_rgb.doc: Convert YUV components using yuv to rgb.
yuv_to_rgb.signature: (y: int, u: int, v: int) -> Any

HaarCascade.doc: Load a Haar cascade by path or built-in cascade name.

load_descriptor.doc: Load an image descriptor from a file.

save_descriptor.doc: Save an image descriptor to a file.

open.doc: Open a playback or capture stream.
open.signature: (self, rate: int, channels: int, format: int, input: bool = False, output: bool = False, input_device_index: Any = None, output_device_index: Any = None, enable_codec: bool = True, frames_per_buffer: int = 1024, start: bool = True, stream_callback: Any = None) -> Any
